content:
The best tools do not make you look busy. They remove friction from the work that already matters.

15 min read
1 hour ago

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

15 Free Tools That Make Developers More ProductiveI used to think developer productivity came from learning more shortcuts, installing more extensions, and making the terminal look impressive.
Then I worked on larger projects.
The kind where productivity was not about typing faster. It was about finding the right file quickly, understanding a messy code path, debugging a real browser issue, checking an API contract, reviewing a pull request without losing context, testing a critical flow, formatting code before review, and explaining a technical decision clearly enough that another developer could trust it.
That changed how I think about tools.
A good developer tool is not just something that saves a few seconds. A good tool reduces repeated friction. It keeps small problems from becoming daily interruptions. It makes the feedback loop shorter. It helps you move from confusion to evidence faster.
The mistake many developers make is collecting tools without improving their workflow. They install ten extensions, five API clients, three terminals, and a dozen dashboards, but the actual development process stays chaotic…
